commit.message:
fix(pdf-ocr): chunked PDFs producing empty OCR results (#169)

* Initial plan

* fix(pdf-ocr): save chunks with useObjectStreams:false and validate PDF header

- Use `{ useObjectStreams: false }` when saving PDF chunks with pdf-lib, producing
  PDF 1.4-compatible output (no cross-reference streams) that is more broadly
  supported by OCR engines such as Mistral OCR via OpenRouter.
- Add `%PDF-` magic-byte header validation immediately after each chunk is saved.
  If pdf-lib fails to produce a valid PDF, a clear error is thrown showing the
  chunk number and page range, instead of sending malformed bytes to the OCR API
  and receiving an opaque empty-content response.
- Update existing multi-chunk test: mock save() to return bytes starting with
  `%PDF-1.4` so the new header validation passes, and assert that save() is
  called with `{ useObjectStreams: false }`.
- Add new test: invalid chunk bytes (all zeros, no PDF header) produce a
  descriptive error message identifying which chunk failed.

Co-authored-by: merlinbecker <3381241+merlinbecker@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix settings code blocks using Bootstrap red instead of theme colors (#331)

## Problem
The \<code>\ elements inside \.section-desc\ in the Settings page (e.g.
\origin/main\, \~/.polypilot/plugins/\, \plugin.json\) were inheriting
Bootstrap's \--bs-code-color\ (\#d63384\, pinkish-red) because no
explicit \color\ was set in the CSS. This was jarring and hard to read
in Dark mode.

## Root Cause
Bootstrap sets \code { color: var(--bs-code-color); }\ globally with
\--bs-code-color: #d63384\. The chat output had already been fixed to
override this with \ ar(--text-bright)\ in \ChatMessageList.razor.css\,
but \.section-desc code\ in \Settings.razor.css\ only had \ ackground\
and \padding\ — no color override.

## Fix
Added \color: var(--text-bright)\ and \ order: 1px solid
var(--border-subtle)\ to \.section-desc code\ in \Settings.razor.css\,
matching the approach used in \ChatMessageList.razor.css\ for markdown
code blocks.

## Tests
- 7 new unit tests in \SettingsCodeBlockThemeTests.cs\ verifying theme
variables are used and Bootstrap red is not present
- 1 new UI scenario in \mode-switch-scenarios.json\ to verify computed
styles at runtime

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Make HasUsedToolsThisTurn volatile for ARM memory model safety (#344)

## Problem

`HasUsedToolsThisTurn` on `SessionState` is read by the watchdog timer
thread and SDK background threads while being written from the UI thread
and SDK event handlers. It had **inconsistent synchronization**: 5 sites
used `Volatile.Read/Write` while 16 used plain access.

On ARM (iOS/Android), plain writes from the UI thread may not be
immediately visible to background thread reads. This is a theoretical
data race that could cause the watchdog to use incorrect timeouts (120s
vs 600s).

Identified during code review of PR #342 (INV-7 from the
processing-state-safety skill).

## Fix

- Declare the field `volatile bool` — all reads/writes automatically
have correct memory barriers
- Remove the 5 now-redundant `Volatile.Read/Write` calls (would generate
CS0420 warnings)
- Remove a duplicate write in the reconnect path (line ~2613 was
redundant with ~2639)
- Replace the `HasUsedToolsThisTurn_VolatileConsistency` test with
`HasUsedToolsThisTurn_IsDeclaredVolatile` that uses reflection to verify
the volatile modifier

## Testing

All 2401 tests pass. No CS0420 warnings.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: replace MouseArea with pointer handlers for proper touch zoom/pan

MouseArea steals touch events and prevents PinchHandler from ever
receiving two simultaneous touch points, making pinch-to-zoom
completely broken on Android.

Replace the entire gesture section with Qt 6 pointer handlers:
- PinchHandler: pinch-to-zoom + two-finger pan on touch
  grabPermissions: CanTakeOverFromAnything — steals points from
  DragHandler when a 2nd finger arrives mid-selection-drag
- DragHandler (LMB / single touch): range selection
  ApprovesTakeOverByAnything — yields gracefully to PinchHandler
- DragHandler (RMB / mouse only): desktop pan
- TapHandler (double tap): clear selection
- WheelHandler: mouse/touchpad scroll zoom (unchanged)

Also update hint text: 'pinch=zoom  2-finger=pan  drag=measure'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix snapshot matching regression: prevent silent snapshot updates

The --match-snapshot flag was silently updating snapshots instead of validating
them. Root cause: snapshots were written during markdown generation (before
validation occurred), then validation compared against the file just written,
so the diff always passed.

Fix: Skip file writing during snapshot matching phase. Snapshots are only
written when using -u flag or when auto-generating missing snapshots.

- Modified src/cli.ts to skip file writing when matchSnapshot flag is set
- Added permanent regression test (test/snapshot-regression.test.ts) that
  validates snapshot matching correctly fails on content mismatches
- Updated DEVELOPMENT.md with snapshot matching documentation
- Updated CHANGE_LOG.md with the fix

The regression test ensures this issue cannot recur silently.

All tests pass (168 passed, 0 failed)
All acceptance tests pass (18 passed, 0 failed)
Verify workflow passes including typecheck and snapshot validation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): eliminate home-stats CORS error by requiring explicit API config

Firefox logs Cross-Origin errors to the console before fetch rejects,
so even though home-stats.js catches the error, the console error
listener in CI still captured it and failed the test.

Root cause: home-stats.js assumed localhost:3001 when hostname=localhost,
but CI also runs Hugo on localhost — the game API is never running there.

Changes:
- home-stats.js: drop localhost fallback; only fetch when data-api-base
  attribute is explicitly set on #home-top3 (empty string = no fetch)
- layouts/index.html: inject data-api-base from site.Params.gameApiBase
  (empty by default, so CI never fetches)
- hugo.toml: add gameApiBase param (default: empty string)
- hugo/site/config.local.toml: local dev override setting localhost:3001
  (gitignored — copy and use with: hugo server --config hugo.toml,config.local.toml)
- .gitignore: add config.local.toml
- basic.spec.js: also filter localhost:3001 console errors as defense-in-depth

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: cap unbounded memory growth in 6 subsystems

- HarRecorder: MAX_HAR=10000 JS cap + Rust max_entries eviction
- Console: MAX_CONSOLE=2000 JS cap on all 7 push paths (log/warn/error/info/debug/error-event/unhandledrejection)
- NetworkLog: MAX_NET=5000 via __onecrawl_push_net() helper on all 3 push paths (fetch success/error, XHR)
- Recording: MAX_FRAMES=6000 (10 min @ 10fps) cap on RecordingState.frames
- EventBus journal: Vec→VecDeque for O(1) eviction via pop_front()
- VisionStream observations: Vec→VecDeque for O(1) eviction, .last()→.back(), slice→iter().skip()

commit.message:
security: harden REST API against DoS and out-of-range inputs

- Add MAX_HEADER_BYTES (16 KB) guard: disconnect any client whose
  accumulated headers exceed the limit before \r\n\r\n is found,
  preventing unbounded buffer growth (Slowloris-style DoS).
- Validate Content-Length: reject requests with negative values,
  non-numeric strings, or bodies > MAX_BODY_BYTES (4 KB); all our
  legitimate request bodies are small JSON objects.
- Clamp voltage/current in PUT /api/v1/setpoint to [0, nomVoltage]
  and [0, nomCurrent] so a malicious client cannot command the PSU
  to a value outside its hardware-rated range.
- Clamp OVP/OCP in PUT /api/v1/limits the same way.
- Clamp ?minutes in GET /api/v1/history to [1/60, 1440] (1 s – 24 h);
  validate that toDouble() succeeded before accepting the value,
  preventing negative-time underflow and infinite FP values.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(dj): sync button now phase-aligns without jumping to start

The phaseAlign() algorithm was broken — it snapped to the nearest beat
then added the other deck's phase offset, which could seek far from the
current position (often back to 0). Rewrote to compute the minimal time
nudge needed: get both decks' beat phases, find the shortest phase
difference (always ≤ half a beat period), and apply that tiny offset.

Sync button now does BPM match + phase align in one click, ready for
a perfect beat-matched crossfade.
